cont_clean: Custody & Child Tax Credit--Just because you have custody of your children doesn't automatically mean you get the Child Tax Credit. Here are 3 things to know 👇 1️⃣ The IRS doesn't track custody: The government doesn't know who has custody. Whoever claims the child on their tax return gets the credit—based on what's filed. 2️⃣ Divorce agreements matter: Even if you have custody, a divorce decree can assign the Child Tax Credit to the other parent. This must be reflected correctly on your return—or it will be rejected. 3️⃣ Never assume the other parent won't claim it: Even if you haven't seen your ex in years, double-claiming triggers IRS audits for both parents. Confirm who's claiming the credit before filing. This content is for informational purposes, not for legal advice or an attorney-client relationship.
